title:
Commentary on Membership
(Members data presented are as of May 31, 2026)
content:
Current membership remained flat at 4,274 members during the period ending March 31, 2026. This represents a year-over-year increase of 0.2% and a quarter-over-quarter decline of -0.8%. The number of potential members stood at 12,661.

The ratio of current members to full-time employees has stabilized in recent quarters after an initial period of adjustment following the previous fiscal year's significant contraction. This alignment indicates that staffing levels are now appropriately matched with service demand for a stable membership base.

Shares per member have declined over this reporting cycle, as evidenced by the downward trend visible since YE'24 and continuing into Q1 2026. When shares-per-member decline, existing members hold smaller average deposit balances; consequently, even though total headcount is unchanged from last year, the funding base available to support operations per member has contracted.

The period-specific assessment shows that while membership count remains stable, efficiency metrics have improved following prior adjustments and deposit depth per member continues to contract. Continued focus on engagement and retention will be necessary to mitigate the impact of declining balances within a static headcount environment.
